seo.title: Amazon Bedrock Agents Guardrails for Fintech
seo.description: Compliance guide for Amazon Bedrock Agents agents in fintech. Meet SOX, PCI DSS requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Fintech teams deploying Amazon Bedrock Agents face heightened compliance risks due to handling sensitive financial data, transaction processing, and regulatory oversight from SOX, PCI DSS, and SEC rules. Without robust guardrails, agents can generate unauthorized financial advice, expose unencrypted PII, or fail to maintain auditable decision trails, leading to severe penalties and loss of banking licenses. Vex provides post-processing verification for Bedrock Agent responses, ensuring compliance with industry-specific policies before delivery to users.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires accurate financial reporting, internal controls, and audit trails for all automated decision-making systems processing transactions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Verifies agent responses contain SOX-compliant disclaimers, audit logging metadata, and flags unapproved financial modifications.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates encryption of cardholder data at rest and in transit, with strict access controls for payment processing agents.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Scans Bedrock Agent outputs for unencrypted PAN, tokenizes sensitive fields, and blocks responses violating PCI scoping requirements.

complianceRequirements.description: Demands explicit consent for PII processing, right to explanation, and data minimization in AI-driven financial services.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Enforces GDPR consent checks, redacts personal data from agent responses, and generates explainability summaries for automated decisions.

complianceRequirements.description: Financial advice from agents must prioritize customer interests with clear disclosures and avoid misleading recommendations.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Validates all investment advice contains Reg BI disclaimers and flags biased or undisclosed conflict recommendations.

industryRisks.name: Unauthorized Financial Advice
industryRisks.description: Bedrock Agent provides investment recommendations without required SEC disclaimers or suitability assessments.
industryRisks.example: Agent suggests high-risk crypto allocation to retail investor without Reg BI disclosure, triggering SEC enforcement.

industryRisks.name: Unencrypted Transaction Data Exposure
industryRisks.description: Action group Lambda returns full PAN or account numbers in plaintext responses.
industryRisks.example: Customer balance inquiry leaks 16-digit card numbers, violating PCI DSS and causing $100K+ fines.

industryRisks.name: Knowledge Base Chunking Misses AML Patterns
industryRisks.description: Retrieval-augmented generation fails to surface suspicious transaction patterns due to poor document chunking.
industryRisks.example: Structuring detection bypassed, leading to BSA/AML violation and banking license suspension.

industryRisks.name: Session State Loss in Multi-Turn Credit Decisions
industryRisks.description: Agent loses conversation context between invocations, generating inconsistent credit risk assessments.
industryRisks.example: Credit approval flips from 'approved' to 'denied' mid-session without explanation, violating fair lending laws.

implementation.title: Wrap Bedrock Agent Invocation with Vex Verification
implementation.description: Post-process all invoke_agent() responses to catch malformed Lambda outputs and enforce Fintech compliance policies.
implementation.code:
import boto3
import vex

bedrock = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key='your-vex-key')

agent_response = bedrock.invoke_agent(
    agentId='your-agent-id',
    agentAliasId='your-alias-id',
    sessionId='session-123',
    inputText='Check my account balance'
)

response_text = agent_response['completion'].read().decode()

# Vex verification with Fintech policy
result = vex_client.verify(
    input='Check my account balance',
    output=response_text,
    policy='fintech-bedrock-agent',
    context={'session_id': 'session-123', 'user_type': 'retail'}
)

if result.valid:
    print(result.output)
else:
    print('Blocked: ' + result.issues[0].message)

implementation.title: Guardrails for Action Group Responses
implementation.description: Intercept Lambda function outputs from action groups to enforce PCI DSS encryption and SOX audit requirements.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Amazon Bedrock Agents in Fintech: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
WealthBridge Financial deployed a Bedrock Agent to handle retail investment inquiries, account management, and transaction approvals. The agent integrated action groups for real-time portfolio analysis via Lambda functions and a knowledge base containing SEC regulations, AML patterns, and customer KYC profiles. During peak trading hours, customers asked complex questions like 'Should I move my 401k into crypto?' or 'Process $10K wire to this account.'

Without compliance guardrails, the agent frequently returned responses missing Reg BI disclaimers, exposed partial account numbers from Lambda action groups, and failed to detect structuring patterns across multi-turn sessions due to chunking issues in the knowledge base. A single Lambda malfunction exposed 500 customer PANs in plaintext, while inconsistent credit decisions across sessions violated fair lending requirements.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After implementing Vex verification wrappers around invoke_agent(), WealthBridge achieved 100% compliance across 250K monthly interactions. Vex blocked 3.2% of responses containing violations like missing SOX audit trails (1.1%), unencrypted PCI data (0.8%), and unauthorized financial advice (1.3%). Multi-turn session state was preserved with context-aware policies, reducing credit decision inconsistencies by 97%. The platform passed its annual SEC audit with zero findings and avoided $2.3M in potential fines.
